data.title: Is Moderna (MRNA) Attractive After A 73% Year To Date Share Price Jump?
data.description:
• Wondering if Moderna at US$53.54 is a bargain or a value trap? This article walks through what the numbers are really saying about the stock.
  • The share price return sits at 2.2% over 7 days, 6.5% over 30 days, 73.5% year to date and 70.1% over the last year, alongside a 64.0% decline over 3 years and a 59.1% decline over 5 years.
  • Recent headlines around Moderna have focused on its position in the pharmaceutical and biotech space and how investors are reassessing companies in this part of the market. This shifting attention provides important context for the recent share price moves and why valuation has come back into focus for many investors.
  • Despite all of this, Moderna currently records a valuation score of 0 out of 6. The next sections will break down what different valuation approaches suggest about the stock and then look at a more complete framework for thinking about value overall.

Moderna scores just 0/6 on our valuation checks. See what other red flags we found in the full valuation breakdown.

Advertisement

Approach 1: Moderna Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model estimates what a business could be worth by projecting its future cash flows and discounting them back to today using a required rate of return. It looks at the cash the company might generate for shareholders rather than just current earnings.

For Moderna, the latest twelve month free cash flow (FCF) is a loss of about $2.19b. Analyst and extrapolated estimates used in this 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity model suggest FCF remains negative for several years, then turns positive, reaching $254m in 2030. Further out, projections step up gradually into the mid hundreds of millions of dollars per year, all expressed in dollars and then discounted to today.

Putting these cash flow projections together, the model arrives at an estimated intrinsic value of about $27.64 per share. Compared with the recent share price of US$53.54, the DCF output implies the stock is around 93.7% overvalued on this set of assumptions.

Result: OVERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests Moderna may be overvalued by 93.7%. Discover 55 high quality undervalued stocks or create your own screener to find better value opportunities.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Moderna.

Approach 2: Moderna Price vs Sales

For companies where earnings are limited or volatile, the P/S ratio is often more useful than P/E, because it compares the share price with the revenue being generated rather than current profits. Investors typically expect higher P/S multiples when they see stronger growth potential and lower perceived risk, and lower P/S multiples when growth expectations are softer or risks are higher.

Moderna currently trades on a P/S of 10.92x. This is close to the broader Biotechs industry average P/S of 10.84x, and above the peer group average of 4.74x. Simply Wall St also calculates a proprietary “Fair Ratio” for the P/S multiple, which for Moderna is 1.34x. This Fair Ratio reflects factors such as earnings growth assumptions, profit margins, size, industry and specific risks, rather than relying only on simple peer or industry comparisons.

Because the Fair Ratio of 1.34x is well below the current P/S of 10.92x, this framework suggests the shares trade at a richer level than would be implied by those fundamentals.

Result: OVERVALUED

P/S ratios tell one story, but what if the real opportunity lies elsewhere? Start investing in legacies, not executives. Discover our 20 top founder-led companies.

Upgrade Your Decision Making: Choose your Moderna Narrative

Earlier it was mentioned that there is an even better way to think about valuation, and on Simply Wall St that comes through Narratives, where you pick the story you believe about Moderna, link that story to clear forecasts, and then see a fair value you can compare with the current share price.

Put simply, a Narrative is your explanation for what is really driving Moderna, combined with concrete numbers for future revenue, earnings and margins. Instead of just plugging figures into a model, you anchor those figures to a story you actually believe.

Each Narrative on the Community page connects that story to a full forecast and a fair value estimate, then sets it alongside the live share price. This helps you decide whether the gap between price and value is big enough for you to consider buying, holding or selling.

These Narratives update as new data and news arrive. When earnings, regulatory updates or clinical results shift the outlook, the fair value view moves with it rather than sitting frozen in time.

For Moderna, one Community member currently assumes a fair value of about US$12 per share while another sees about US$175 per share. That wide range shows how different stories about mRNA demand, pricing pressure, litigation and the pipeline can lead to very different conclusions about what the stock is worth today.

For Moderna however we will make it really easy for you with previews of two leading Moderna Narratives:

🐂 Moderna Bull Case

Fair value in this Narrative: US$175.00 per share

Implied discount to this fair value vs the latest close: about 69.4% undervalued

Revenue growth used in this Narrative: 48.43%

  • Argues that Moderna used its COVID-19 windfall to build a large pipeline while keeping the balance sheet relatively clean and the shares trading near or below book value.
  • Frames the sharp pullback from the pandemic share price peak as an overreaction during a transition period as pipeline projects progress through trials.
  • Views Moderna as a comparatively lower risk biotech idea for value focused investors, with multiple shots on goal across existing and pipeline products.

🐻 Moderna Bear Case

Implied premium to this fair value vs the latest close: about 38.0% overvalued

  • Sees the expanded mRNA pipeline and cost measures as helpful, but questions whether they fully offset slowing vaccine demand and competition.
  • Highlights execution and policy risks, including pricing pressure, regulatory shifts and reliance on cost cuts and external partners to fund more expensive programs.
  • Points out that the analyst fair value and required future P/E assume meaningful earnings progress and multiple support, which may be hard to justify if key products or trials disappoint.

data.title: Linmon Media Profit Turnaround Tests Bullish Narratives On Earnings Quality
data.description:
Linmon Media (SEHK:9857) has just posted its FY 2025 first half numbers, with revenue of C¥401.3 million and basic EPS of C¥0.03, while trailing twelve month revenue stands at C¥862.5 million and EPS at C¥0.09. Over recent reporting periods, revenue has moved from C¥192.5 million in 1H FY 2024 to C¥464.5 million in 2H FY 2024 and then to C¥401.3 million in 1H FY 2025. This has come alongside a swing from a net loss of C¥52.6 million in 1H FY 2024 and C¥136.6 million in 2H FY 2024 to net income of C¥10.8 million in the latest half, which puts the spotlight firmly on how durable these margins really are.

See our full analysis for Linmon Media.

With the headline figures in place, the next step is to see how this move into profit lines up with the key narratives investors follow around Linmon Media’s growth, risks, and earnings quality.

Curious how numbers become stories that shape markets? Explore Community Narratives

Advertisement

TTM profit of C¥31.2 million relies heavily on non cash items

  • Over the trailing twelve months, Linmon Media reported net income of C¥31.2 million and basic EPS of C¥0.09 on revenue of C¥862.5 million, so current profit is still modest against the revenue base.
  • Critics highlight that the bearish concern around earnings quality is backed up by the data, as the move from a loss of C¥189.1 million in the earlier trailing period to a profit of C¥31.2 million comes with a high proportion of non cash components, which makes it harder to treat the new profitability as firmly established.
    • What stands out for a bearish view is that the trailing period just before this one also showed revenue above C¥850 million but still produced a loss of C¥125.8 million, so the swing in net income is not clearly tied to a large change in scale.
    • Given that the risk summary singles out non cash earnings as a major issue, the recent profit level of C¥10.8 million in 1H FY 2025 may not fully resolve questions about how repeatable this performance is.

Five year earnings growth of 10.5% vs uneven recent halves

  • Over the last five years, earnings growth is reported at 10.5% per year, yet recent half year figures range from a loss of C¥136.6 million in 2H FY 2024 to a profit of C¥10.8 million in 1H FY 2025, so the long term trend sits beside a choppy recent record.
  • Supporters argue that a cautiously bullish narrative is still intact because multi year earnings growth and the shift into profit are both present, but the sequence of losses of C¥52.6 million and C¥136.6 million before the current C¥10.8 million profit means the path to that 10.5% annual growth has involved sizeable swings.
    • What helps the bullish angle is that trailing EPS of C¥0.09 is positive after earlier periods of negative EPS as low as C¥0.52 loss on a trailing basis, which shows the business has produced positive earnings over the latest twelve months.
    • At the same time, the jump from a basic EPS loss of C¥0.38 in 2H FY 2024 to a small profit of C¥0.03 in 1H FY 2025 suggests bulls need to pay close attention to how much of that turnaround is linked to non cash factors rather than a steady operational shift.

Bulls and skeptics are reading very different stories into the same five year growth rate, and the full context sits in the detailed narratives for Linmon Media. Curious how numbers become stories that shape markets? Explore Community Narratives

29.7x P/E compared with 10.9x industry multiple

  • On trailing numbers, Linmon Media trades on a P/E of 29.7x, which is higher than the Hong Kong Entertainment industry average of 10.9x and well above the peer average of 3.8x, so the current share price of C¥2.90 embeds a materially richer multiple than sector references.
  • Skeptics point out that this relatively high P/E multiple sits next to the flagged risk on earnings quality, creating tension for a bearish view that questions how comfortable investors should be paying 29.7x for profit that includes a large non cash component.
    • What strengthens that cautious stance is that the recent twelve month profit of C¥31.2 million follows a prior trailing period loss of C¥189.1 million, which means the valuation is being set on a profit figure that has not been tested over many periods.
    • With no forward revenue or earnings forecasts provided alongside this P/E, bears focus on the gap between the 29.7x multiple and the 10.9x industry level rather than on any quantified growth expectations that might justify the premium.

Next Steps

Don't just look at this quarter; the real story is in the long-term trend. We've done an in-depth analysis on Linmon Media's growth and its valuation to see if today's price is a bargain. Add the company to your watchlist or portfolio now so you don't miss the next big move.

With both bullish and bearish angles on the table, it helps to look directly at the numbers and decide what they mean for you. To weigh those mixed signals against the company's flagged concerns and potential upsides, take a closer look at the 1 key reward and 1 important warning sign.

See What Else Is Out There

Linmon Media’s recent profit relies heavily on non cash items, sits on a modest base, and is paired with a relatively rich 29.7x P/E against peers.

If you are uneasy about paying up for uncertain earnings quality and a premium multiple, compare this story with companies in the 284 resilient stocks with low risk scores to focus on more resilient profiles.

A Discounted Cash Flow model takes projected future cash flows and discounts them back to today, to estimate what the business might be worth in present value terms.

Approach 1: Oncolytics Biotech Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model estimates what a business could be worth today by projecting its future cash flows and then discounting those back to a present value.

For Oncolytics Biotech, the model used is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach based on cash flow projections. The company currently reports last twelve month free cash flow of CA$26.48 million in outflows. Analyst inputs and Simply Wall St extrapolations point to projected free cash flows moving to CA$169.4 million by 2030, with a series of negative and then positive figures along the way as shown in the ten year forecast path.

Aggregating these CA$ cash flows and discounting them back gives an estimated intrinsic value of US$87.24 per share. Compared with a share price around US$0.95, this DCF output implies the stock is 98.9% undervalued based solely on these projections and assumptions.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Approach 2: Oncolytics Biotech Price vs Book

For companies that are still building toward consistent profitability, the price to book, or P/B, ratio is often a practical way to think about valuation because it compares what you pay in the market with the accounting value of the business on its balance sheet.

In general, higher growth expectations and lower perceived risk can support a higher “normal” P/B multiple, while slower growth and higher risk usually line up with a lower multiple. That is why simply comparing one stock’s P/B to another’s can be misleading if their growth profiles and risk levels are very different.

Margins and Net Profit Catch Up to Store Growth

  • On a trailing basis, net income excluding extra items is C¥141.9 million on C¥5.4b of revenue, which works out to a 2.6% net margin compared with 1.3% a year earlier.
  • Consensus narrative highlights operational efficiencies and scale benefits as a key support for margins, and the higher trailing margin aligns with that, while store level staff costs at 28% of revenue and rider expenses mentioned in the cautious view show that wage and delivery costs remain an important swing factor for how much of future revenue turns into profit.
    • For example, semi annual net income excluding extra items went from C¥10.9 million in 1H 2024 to C¥65.9 million in 1H 2025 alongside revenue moving from C¥2.0b to C¥2.6b, which is consistent with better profit conversion on a larger base.
    • At the same time, the bearish narrative flags that higher delivery penetration and aggregator commissions could limit further uplift in group EBITDA margin, so the recent 2.6% net margin still sits against those cost headwinds.

Same Store Sales Slip While Network Expands

  • Total restaurants increased from 914 in 1H 2024 to 1,198 in 1H 2025, while same restaurant sales moved from 3.6% growth to a 1% decline and management commentary notes a 5.3% decline in average daily sales per store to C¥12,428.
  • Bears argue that aggressive expansion into non Tier 1 cities, which now contribute 58.8% of revenue, risks weaker unit economics over time, and the combination of a 1% decline in same store sales and lower average daily sales per store directly reflects that concern.
    • The plan to add 307 net openings in 2025 and 350 more in 2026, alongside a reported 12 month payback period for new stores, is framed as attractive today, but bears caution that slower ramp in newer cohorts could stretch those payback periods and weigh on margins.
    • Rider costs and aggregator commissions have already pushed store level staff costs to 28% of revenue, so if same store sales remain under pressure while fixed costs tied to a larger store base keep rising, the cautious view sees a risk that profit growth lags the pace of store openings.
On these store productivity and cost trends, skeptics see plenty of room for debate about how robust earnings will look a few years out, which is exactly what the more cautious narrative unpacks in detail. 🐻 DPC Dash Bear Case

Fast EPS Growth Meets Premium Valuation
